data.content:
### Filter Accounts by EnablePaymentsToAccount

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/how-to-guides/integration-best-practices

This example shows how to filter the list of accounts retrieved from Xero. It specifically looks for accounts where 'EnablePaymentsToAccount' is true and the 'AccountType' is 'BANK', which is necessary for handling payments correctly.

```JavaScript
function filterAccountsForPayments(accounts) {
  return accounts.filter(account => 
    account.EnablePaymentsToAccount && account.AccountType === 'BANK'
  );
}
```

--------------------------------

### Manage Payment Services

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/scopes

Enables viewing and managing payment services within Xero. This scope is for integrating with Xero's payment functionalities.

```Xero API
paymentservices
```

--------------------------------

### Create and Retrieve Batch Payments (Xero API)

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/batchpayments

This snippet demonstrates how to interact with the Xero Batch Payments API to create and retrieve batch payment transactions. It supports GET, PUT, and POST methods. Batch payments allow bundling multiple invoices or bills into a single payment for simplified bank reconciliation.

```HTTP
GET https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BatchPayments
PUT https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BatchPayments
POST https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BatchPayments
```

--------------------------------

### Retrieve Accounts Endpoint

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/how-to-guides/integration-best-practices

This snippet demonstrates how to fetch the user's chart of accounts from the Xero API. It's crucial for populating dropdowns and allowing users to map their accounts correctly. The response includes account details like type and whether payments can be applied.

```JavaScript
import XeroClient from 'xero-node';

const xero = new XeroClient({
  // ... client options
});

async function getAccounts(tenantId) {
  try {
    const accounts = await xero.accountingApi.getAccounts(tenantId);
    console.log(accounts.body.accounts);
    return accounts.body.accounts;
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Error fetching accounts:', error);
    throw error;
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### POST Apply Payment Service

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/brandingthemes

Applies a payment service to a branding theme. Requires the PaymentServiceID. Multiple custom payment services can be associated, but only the last one in the request will be used for online invoices.

```http
POST https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BrandingThemes/{BrandingThemeID}/PaymentServices
```

--------------------------------

### Fetch Accounts Endpoint - Xero API

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api-guides/integration-best-practices

This snippet demonstrates how to retrieve a user's chart of accounts from the Xero API. It's crucial for populating dropdowns and allowing users to map their accounts correctly. The response includes account details like type and whether payments can be applied.

```HTTP
GET https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Accounts

// Example Response Snippet:
{
  "Id": "a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-1234-567890abcdef",
  "Name": "Sales",
  "Code": "750",
  "Type": "REVENUE",
  "EnablePaymentsToAccount": false
},
{
  "Id": "b2c3d4e5-f6a7-8901-2345-67890abcdef0",
  "Name": "Checking Account",
  "Code": "990",
  "Type": "BANK",
  "EnablePaymentsToAccount": true
}
```

--------------------------------

### POST Apply Payment Service Request

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/brandingthemes

Example JSON request body for applying a payment service to a branding theme. It includes an array of PaymentServices, each with a PaymentServiceID.

```json
{
"PaymentServices": [{
"PaymentServiceID": "de5c978d-3cbf-4ebb-9ca9-20d7cb196ab1"  }]
}
```

--------------------------------

### GET Payment Services

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/brandingthemes

Retrieves the payment services applied to a branding theme. The response includes details like PaymentServiceID, PaymentServiceName, PaymentServiceUrl, PayNowText, and PaymentServiceType. The URL can contain placeholders for invoice data.

```http
GET https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BrandingThemes/{BrandingThemeID}/PaymentServices
```

--------------------------------

### Create ACCREC Batch Payment

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/batchpayments

Illustrates how to create an Accounts Receivable (ACCREC) batch payment via the Xero API. This PUT request specifies the date, reference, account, and a list of payments, each linked to an invoice and including an amount.

```HTTP
PUT https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BatchPayments
```

```JSON
{
"Date": "2018-08-01",
"Reference": "Particulars",
"Account": {
"AccountID": "ac993f75-035b-433c-82e0-7b7a2d40802c"  },
"Payments": [
    {
"Reference": "Something",
"Invoice": {
"InvoiceID": "d8ec835f-fef6-4d5c-ae41-28df59c57f11"      },
"Amount": 100    },
    {
"Reference": "Something else",
"Invoice": {
"InvoiceID": "58ec1ad5-5f69-423c-b32e-706a29c13248"      },
"Amount": 200    }
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### GET Payment Services Response

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/brandingthemes

Example JSON response for retrieving payment services. It lists payment services with their IDs, names, and types. Custom services may include a URL and specific text for the 'Pay Now' button.

```json
{
"PaymentServices": [{
"PaymentServiceID": "0751da5f-8b28-4f93-b05e-1b85bedfd900",
"PaymentServiceName": "DONOTPAYTHISISFAKE@fakedemoaccount.com",
"PaymentServiceType": "PayPal"  },
  {
"PaymentServiceID": "7f0f43b1-9ba9-4ba4-a785-e677652c7d7e",
"PaymentServiceName": "Awesome Pay",
"PaymentServiceUrl": "https://www.awesomepay.com/?invoiceNo=[INVOICENUMBER]&currency=[CURRENCY]&amount=[AMOUNTDUE]&shortCode=[SHORTCODE]",
"PayNowText": "Pay via AwesomePay",
"PaymentServiceType": "Custom"
}]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Create ACCPAY Batch Payment (NZ)

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/batchpayments

Provides an example of creating an Accounts Payable (ACCPAY) batch payment using the Xero API. This PUT request includes details like date, particulars, code, reference, account information, and a list of individual payments with invoice references and amounts.

```HTTP
PUT https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BatchPayments
```

```JSON
{
"Date": "2018-08-01",
"Particulars": "paying",
"Code": "333",
"Reference": "ddd",
"Account": {
"AccountID": "ac993f75-035b-433c-82e0-7b7a2d40802c"  },
"Payments": [
    {
"BankAccountNumber": "123-456-7890",
"Particulars": "def",
"Code": "543",
"Reference": "ggg",
"Invoice": {
"InvoiceID": "d8ec835f-fef6-4d5c-ae41-28df59c57f11"      },
"Amount": 10    },
    {
"BankAccountNumber": "123-456-7890",
"Particulars": "abc",
"Code": "123",
"Reference": "fff",
"Invoice": {
"InvoiceID": "71d1c9ca-3e99-4ef5-8f44-19d24c500ac8"      },
"Amount": 10    }
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Add Notes to a Batch Payment (JSON)

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/batchpayments

This snippet illustrates how to add notes to a batch payment using a PUT request to the Xero API. It involves providing an array of HistoryRecords, where each record contains details for the note.

```HTTP
PUT https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Payments/{Guid}/History

```

```JSON
{
"HistoryRecords": [
    {
"Details": "Note added by your favourite app!"
    }
    ...
  ]
}

```

--------------------------------

### Retrieve a Specific Batch Payment

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/batchpayments

This snippet shows how to retrieve a single batch payment by appending its unique BatchPaymentID to the API endpoint.

```http
GET https://.../BatchPayments/297c2dc5-cc47-4afd-8ec8-74990b8761e9
```

--------------------------------

### Allocate Credit Notes API

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/creditnotes

Allows allocation of credit notes to invoices. This functionality is managed through the payments endpoint.

```bash
POST https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/Payments
```

--------------------------------

### Retrieve Batch Payments by Type

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/batchpayments

This snippet demonstrates how to retrieve batch payments filtered by their type, specifically 'PAYBATCH'. It utilizes the 'where' parameter for optimized filtering.

```http
GET https://.../BatchPayments?where=Type="PAYBATCH"
```

--------------------------------

### Xero Invoice URL Field Example

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/how-to-guides/integration-best-practices

Demonstrates how to use the `<Url>` field in the Xero Invoices endpoint to link back to an external application from an invoice within Xero. The URL cannot contain the word 'xero'.

```XML
<Invoice>
  <Url>http://my-application.com/invoice/123</Url>
  ...
</Invoice>
```

--------------------------------

### Retrieve Authorized Batch Payments

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/batchpayments

This snippet demonstrates how to retrieve batch payments that have an 'AUTHORISED' status. It uses the 'where' parameter for efficient filtering.

```http
GET https://.../BatchPayments?where=Status="AUTHORISED"
```

--------------------------------

### Retrieve Batch Payments by Account ID

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/batchpayments

This snippet shows how to retrieve batch payments associated with a specific account ID. It uses the 'where' parameter with a GUID to filter the results.

```http
GET https://.../BatchPayments?where=Account.AccountId=guid("13918178-849a-4823-9a31-57b7eac713d7")
```

--------------------------------

### Financial Services Approval Form

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/how-to-guides/app-partner-steps

For financial services apps (banking, payments, lending, insurance) requiring Section 11 approval, complete the designated form. The link to the form is provided.

```url
this form
```

--------------------------------

### Retrieve Individual Batch Payment (NZ)

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/batchpayments

Demonstrates how to retrieve details of a specific batch payment using its ID via the Xero API. This GET request returns a JSON object containing batch payment information, including associated payments and account details.

```HTTP
GET https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BatchPayments/54fb5c27-b2bc-4d54-a92f-a86b765d71c1
```

```JSON
{
"BatchPayments": [
    {
"Account": {
"AccountID": "13918178-849a-4823-9a31-57b7eac713d7",
"CurrencyCode": "NZD"
      },
"Reference": "ref",
"BatchPaymentID": "44a1013e-4946-4a73-b207-dfe5424a5ea5",
"DateString": "2018-10-03T00:00:00",
"Date": "/Date(1538524800000+0000)/",
"Payments": [
        {
"Invoice": {
"InvoiceID": "5aa9451d-95d1-4f95-a966-bbab2573f71c",
"Payments": [],
"CreditNotes": [],
"Prepayments": [],
"Overpayments": [],
"HasErrors": false,
"IsDiscounted": false,
"LineItems": [],
"CurrencyCode": "NZD"
          },
"PaymentID": "a22a64cb-364e-43fa-9a1f-bb2cd1f4adde",
"Reference": "ref/cheque",
"Amount": 913.55,
"BankAmount": 913.55
        },
        {
"Invoice": {
"InvoiceID": "30a87092-31b5-4a2c-831e-327486533dd2",
"Payments": [],
"CreditNotes": [],
"Prepayments": [],
"Overpayments": [],
"HasErrors": false,
"IsDiscounted": false,
"LineItems": [],
"CurrencyCode": "AUD"
          },
"PaymentID": "6e20be79-32d8-4ae1-978e-f76d9b245c02",
"Amount": 495,
"BankAmount": 540
        },
        {
"Invoice": {
"InvoiceID": "86d6e00f-ef56-49f7-9a54-796ccd5ca057",
"Payments": [],
"CreditNotes": [],
"Prepayments": [],
"Overpayments": [],
"HasErrors": false,
"IsDiscounted": false,
"LineItems": [],
"CurrencyCode": "NZD"
          },
"PaymentID": "4ba761b8-5940-4a3f-bcdf-7775adb00332",
"Amount": 3080,
"BankAmount": 3080
        }
      ],
"Type": "RECBATCH",
"Status": "AUTHORISED",

    }

```

--------------------------------

### Invoice URL Field Example

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api-guides/integration-best-practices

Demonstrates the usage of the URL field within the Xero Invoices API to create a link on an invoice that navigates back to the developer's application. This helps users identify the source of the invoice and provides easy access to related information.

```XML
<Invoices>
  <Invoice>
    <Type>ACCREC</Type>
    <ContactGroup>
      <ContactID>4bb29079-7004-471f-a772-000000000000</ContactID>
    </ContactGroup>
    <Date>2023-01-01</Date>
    <DueDate>2023-01-31</DueDate>
    <LineAmountTypes>Exclusive</LineAmountTypes>
    <LineItems>
      <LineItem>
        <Description>Monthly retainer fee</Description>
        <Quantity>1</Quantity>
        <UnitAmount>500.00</UnitAmount>
        <AccountCode>300</AccountCode>
        <TaxType>AU-GST</TaxType>
      </LineItem>
    </LineItems>
    <Url>http://mycompany.mydomain.com/invoices/12345</Url>
  </Invoice>
</Invoices>
```

--------------------------------

### Update Batch Payment Status to DELETED (JSON)

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/batchpayments

This snippet shows how to update the status of a batch payment to DELETED using a POST request to the Xero API. It requires the BatchPaymentID and the desired Status.

```HTTP
POST https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BatchPayments

```

```JSON
{
"BatchPaymentID": "9bf296e9-0748-4d29-a3dc-24dde1098030",
"Status": "DELETED"
}

```

--------------------------------

### Update Batch Payment Status to DELETED (ID in URL)

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/batchpayments

This snippet demonstrates updating a batch payment's status to DELETED by including the BatchPaymentID directly in the URL of the POST request to the Xero API.

```HTTP
POST https://api.xero.com/api.xro/2.0/BatchPayments/9bf296e9-0748-4d29-a3dc-24dde1098030

```

```JSON
{
"Status": "DELETED"
}

```

--------------------------------

### Xero API: Accounting Transactions Scopes

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/scopes

Scopes for managing and viewing business transactions within the Xero Accounting API. This includes invoices, quotes, payments, and more. Some scopes allow read-only access.

```text
accounting.transactions: View and manage your business transactions | BankTransactions, BankTransfers, BatchPayments, CreditNotes, ExpenseClaims, Invoices, LinkedTransactions, ManualJournals, Overpayments, Quotes, Payments, Prepayments, PurchaseOrders, Receipts, RepeatingInvoices
accounting.transactions.read: View your business transactions | _As above but GET only_
```

--------------------------------

### Manage UK Payroll Employees

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/scopes

Allows viewing and managing employee data within the UK payroll system. This includes access to employment details, leave balances, and payment information.

```Xero API
payroll.employees
```

--------------------------------

### Create Xero Invoice for COGS Adjustment

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/how-to-guides/how-to-integrate-my-inventory-system-with-xero

This snippet demonstrates how to create a zero-total invoice in Xero to record the reduction of stock on hand and the increase in Cost of Goods Sold (COGS). This is useful for updating inventory values and reflecting sales costs accurately in financial reports.

```json
{
  "Type": "ACCREC",
  "Contact": {
    "ContactID": "<contact_id>"
  },
  "LineItems": [
    {
      "Description": "Cost of Goods Sold Adjustment",
      "Quantity": 1,
      "UnitAmount": 0,
      "AccountCode": "<cogs_account_code>",
      "LineAmount": 0,
      "TaxType": "NONE"
    },
    {
      "Description": "Stock on Hand Adjustment",
      "Quantity": 1,
      "UnitAmount": 0,
      "AccountCode": "<stock_on_hand_account_code>",
      "LineAmount": 0,
      "TaxType": "NONE"
    }
  ],
  "Status": "AUTHORISED"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Integrate with Xero using SDKs

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/getting-started-guide

Leverage Xero's Software Development Kits (SDKs) and sample applications to accelerate your integration process. These resources provide pre-built components and examples for common tasks.

```Python
from xero_python_sdk.api import AccountingApi

# Initialize API client
api_client = AccountingApi(api_client=xero_client)

# Get organisations
organisations = api_client.get_organisations()
print(organisations)
```

```Node.js
const { AccountingApi } = require('xero-node');

// Initialize API client
const accountingApi = new AccountingApi(xeroClient);

// Get organisations
accountingApi.getOrganisations().then(organisations => {
  console.log(organisations);
}).catch(err => {
  console.error(err);
});

```

--------------------------------

### Manage eInvoicing Registration

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/guides/oauth2/scopes

Enables viewing and managing eInvoicing registration information within Xero. This scope is for integrating with eInvoicing services.

```Xero API
einvoicing
```

--------------------------------

### Submit Expense Claims (HTTP POST)

Source: https://developer.xero.com/documentation/api/accounting/expenseclaims

This section describes the POST method for submitting one or many expense claims to the Xero API. It notes that payments for expense claims must be handled within the Xero application itself.

```http
POST ExpenseClaims
```
